human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for EV-specific dealer and warranty execution fidelity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, battery traceability and release-boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to EV dealer, warranty, and product-service execution with VIN, recall, diagnostic, battery, and return-flow evidence requirements.

Authority sources cover NHTSA EV safety, towing and storage guidance, recall and manufacturer-communications records, VIN decoding, AIAG supply-chain and warranty references, and OEM public EV warranty or service bulletins.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, conflicting-policy escalation, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/ev-operations-specialist/ so EV operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and engineering lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering diagnosis, supplier approval, safety release, dangerous-goods legal interpretation, recall command, and financial exception authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated EV operations specialist for dealer execution, warranty progression, high-voltage safety-aware service handling, battery traceability, and audit-ready completion evidence capture.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
